reasoning: Both unsafe findings are false positives: HTML entities in onedrive_service.sh are harmless syntax errors, and onedrive.init's PATH/script usage follows standard init practices. No actual malicious behavior exists.
disagreement_analysis: qwen model misinterpreted harmless syntax quirks as obfuscation and standard init script patterns as security risks.
coverage_issues: Source code (v2.5.10.tar.gz) skipped by both models: critical for detecting runtime risks like network exfiltration
PKGBUILD.in not reviewed by qwen despite being referenced in build process
false_positives: qwen: HTML entities (") in onedrive_service.sh - syntax error not obfuscation
qwen: PATH=/usr/local/bin in onedrive.init - common init script pattern
learnings: Prioritize source code review: Models skipped critical source tarball containing executable logic

Adjust file selection: PKGBUILD.in should be higher priority as it influences build
Reduce false positives: Train models to recognize harmless syntax quirks vs true obfuscation
